title: Savory Sweet Ukrainian Grape Juice Confit Tomato Pilaf
description: A dense and flavorful Ukrainian dish featuring grape juice and confit tomatoes, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This ovo-vegetarian friendly meal is made with simple ingredients and can be prepared in advance.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large saucepan, heat the oil over medium heat and saute the onions until they are translucent.
Add the grated carrots to the saucepan and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked rice, confit tomatoes, grape juice, salt, and pepper, and stir until the rice is well coated.
In a separate bowl, beat the eggs and set them aside.
Add the egg mixture to the rice mixture and stir until everything is well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and top with chopped parsley.
Bake the pilaf in the preheated oven for 30 minutes, or until the eggs are set and the top is lightly browned.
Remove the pilaf from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
